Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING START
=======================

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

}\\n            }\\n            // 贏分位置跳過（接下來馬上會被 EliminatePos 隱藏+特效蓋住）\\n            if (!eliminateSet.has(position)) {\\n                const col = Math.floor(position / Game_Define.ROW);\\n ```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Target spine layer inconsistency in PlayTargetExplodeEffect</title>
  <subtitle>Reused target spines remain in SYMBOL layer instead of SCATTER layer during explosion animation</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>PlayTargetExplodeEffect function reuses existing target spine nodes from m_targetStopNodes map when available</fact>
    <fact>ReelStopEffect places target spines in Game_Define.LAYERS.SYMBOL layer at line 1199</fact>
    <fact>PlayTargetExplodeEffect fallback path correctly uses Game_Define.LAYERS.SCATTER layer at line 1195</fact>
    <fact>Reused spine path (lines 1183-1192) plays animation without correcting parent layer assignment</fact>
    <fact>Layer mismatch causes visual rendering order errors between reused and fallback target explosions</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The PlayTargetExplodeEffect function has two code paths for displaying target explosion animations. When a target spine already exists at a position (stored in m_targetStopNodes from ReelStopEffect), it reuses that node and plays the "Active" animation. However, ReelStopEffect initially places these spines in the SYMBOL layer (line 1199: `symbolNode.setParent(this.m_effectNodes[Game_Define.LAYERS.SYMBOL])`), and the reuse path doesn't correct this. The fallback path, used when no existing spine is found, correctly assigns the new node to the SCATTER layer (line 1195). This inconsistency means reused target explosions render at the wrong z-order compared to fallback explosions, potentially appearing behind or in front of other elements incorrectly.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>assets/Script/EffectPlate/EffectPlate.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```

```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Game layer hierarchy defines five rendering layers</title>
  <subtitle>LAYERS constant defines z-order: SYMBOL(0), PRIATE(1), TARGET(2), SCATTER(3), MULTI_FLY(4)</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Game_Define.LAYERS.SYMBOL is layer 0 (lowest rendering order)</fact>
    <fact>Game_Define.LAYERS.PRIATE is layer 1</fact>
    <fact>Game_Define.LAYERS.TARGET is layer 2</fact>
    <fact>Game_Define.LAYERS.SCATTER is layer 3</fact>
    <fact>Game_Define.LAYERS.MULTI_FLY is layer 4 (highest rendering order)</fact>
    <fact>Higher layer numbers render on top of lower layer numbers</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The Game_Define.ts file defines a LAYERS constant that establishes the visual z-order hierarchy for all game effects. The five layers range from SYMBOL (0) at the bottom to MULTI_FLY (4) at the top. This layer system determines which visual elements appear in front of or behind others. The target explosion bug occurs because spines remain in SYMBOL layer (0) when they should be promoted to SCATTER layer (3) or TARGET layer (2), causing them to render behind elements they should appear in front of during explosion animations.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>assets/Script/Game_Define.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Target explosion sequence coordination with character and cannon animations</title>
  <subtitle>PlayTargetExplodeEffect awaits after 0.5s delay, triggering cannon and character animations 1s later</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>CountMultState waits 0.5s before calling PlayTargetExplodeEffect at line 191</fact>
    <fact>Cannon and character Symbol_Remove animations scheduled 1.0s after explosion starts (line 183-188)</fact>
    <fact>isExpanded flag determines explosion type based on FreeGame mode and FreePickItems containing 0</fact>
    <fact>After target explosion completes, state transitions to EXPLODE state (line 198)</fact>
    <fact>Target explosion is awaited, blocking state progression until animation completes</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The target explosion effect is carefully orchestrated within the multiplier counting phase. After a 0.5-second setup delay, PlayTargetExplodeEffect is called with await, blocking state progression. One second into the explosion, supporting animations (cannon firing and character actions) are triggered non-blockingly via scheduleOnce. The isExpanded parameter controls whether gold targets (Active_Gold) or normal targets (Active_Red) are used, determined by free game mode and player pick selections. Only after the full explosion animation completes does the game transition to the EXPLODE state for symbol elimination. The layer bug would cause these timed explosions to render incorrectly when reusing existing target spines.</narrative>

    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

content.thinking:
Now I can see the exact bug location. Looking at lines 710-720 (the reuse path) vs lines 722-737 (the fallback path):

**Reuse path (lines 710-720):**
- Gets existing node from `m_targetStopNodes`
- Plays "Active" animation directly on the existing node
- **Does NOT change the parent layer** - the node stays wherever it was placed (in SYMBOL layer from ReelStopEffect line 1199)

**Fallback path (lines 722-737):**
- Gets new node from pool
- **Sets parent to `this.m_effectNodes[Game_Define.LAYERS.SCATTER]`** at line 726
- Sets world position
- Plays "Active" animation

The bug is clear: the reuse path doesn't call `setParent()` to move the node to the SCATTER layer, so it remains in the SYMBOL layer (0) instead of SCATTER layer (3), causing incorrect z-ordering.

The fix would be to add:
```typescript
stopNodeInfo.node.setParent(this.m_effectNodes[Game_Define.LAYERS.SCATTER]);
```
before playing the animation in the reuse path.

content.text:
```xml
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Exact location of layer bug in target explosion reuse path</title>
  <subtitle>Lines 710-720 reuse path missing setParent call that fallback path has at line 726</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Reuse path (lines 710-720) retrieves existing target node and plays Active animation without changing parent layer</fact>
    <fact>Fallback path (lines 722-737) explicitly sets parent to SCATTER layer at line 726 via symbolNode.setParent</fact>
    <fact>Reused nodes remain in SYMBOL layer (0) from original ReelStopEffect placement</fact>
    <fact>Fallback nodes correctly placed in SCATTER layer (3) ensuring proper z-order</fact>
    <fact>Both paths play identical Active animation but with different render layer assignments</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The bug manifests in the divergent handling of layer assignment between two code paths in PlayTargetExplodeEffect. When reusing an existing target spine from m_targetStopNodes (the common case), lines 710-720 directly play the "Active" animation on the node without reassigning its parent layer. These nodes were originally placed in the SYMBOL layer (0) by ReelStopEffect at line 1199. In contrast, the fallback path for newly-spawned nodes (lines 722-737) explicitly calls `symbolNode.setParent(this.m_effectNodes[Game_Define.LAYERS.SCATTER])` at line 726, correctly placing them in SCATTER layer (3). This inconsistency causes reused target explosions to render at the wrong z-depth, appearing behind elements they should be in front of. The fix requires adding the missing setParent call in the reuse path before animation playback.</narrative>

```xml
<observation>
  <type>bugfix</type>
  <title>Fixed target explosion layer inconsistency by reparenting reused nodes to SCATTER layer</title>
  <subtitle>Reused target spines now correctly moved from SYMBOL to SCATTER layer before playing explosion animation</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added setParent call to move reused target nodes to Game_Define.LAYERS.SCATTER before animation playback</fact>
    <fact>Added setWorldPosition call to ensure reused nodes maintain correct position after reparenting</fact>
    <fact>Fix applied at lines 714-717 in PlayTargetExplodeEffect function</fact>
    <fact>Reuse path now matches fallback path layer assignment behavior</fact>
    <fact>Comment added explaining reused nodes originate from SYMBOL layer in ReelStopEffect</fact>
    <fact>Fix prevents target explosion effects from being incorrectly covered by other elements</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The layer bug in PlayTargetExplodeEffect has been fixed by adding the missing layer reassignment logic to the reuse path. When an existing target spine node is reused from m_targetStopNodes, it was originally placed in the SYMBOL layer (layer 0) by ReelStopEffect. The explosion animation needs these nodes to render at the correct z-depth (SCATTER layer 3) to appear above other effects. The fix adds `stopNodeInfo.node.setParent(this.m_effectNodes[Game_Define.LAYERS.SCATTER])` to reparent the node to the SCATTER layer, and `stopNodeInfo.node.setWorldPosition(worldPos)` to maintain correct positioning after the reparent operation. This brings the reuse path into consistency with the fallback path, which already correctly assigned new nodes to the SCATTER layer. An explanatory comment in Chinese documents why this reparenting is necessary, referencing the original SYMBOL layer placement in ReelStopEffect.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>problem-solution</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/EffectPlate/EffectPlate.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
